California
Fair POlitical
Practices Commission
November f2, L99l
Michael R. Barrette′
 Attorney
Refuge Bus■ness PlaZa
■521 Butte House Road′
 Suite
Yuba City′  CA 95993

at LaW

Your Request for AdviCe
Our File No. I-9■
-497
Dear Hr. Barrette:
You are seeking advice on behalf of Butte County Supervisor
Ed Mcl,aughlin with respect to his duties and obligations under the
confLict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
rrgsgrrl.l The following advice is based upon the analysis you have
provided in your letter of october 2L, L991, a letter dated
October 25, LggL from NeiI I'lccabe, Butte County Deputy County
Counsel, a telephone conversation with you on Novenber L, 1991,
and telephone conversations $/ith DIr. llccabe on october 31,
November 1, and November 6, 1991. While your actuaL client-is
Site Construction, Inc., whon you rePresent before the Board of
Supervisors concerning the use-permit application at issue in this
.utt.., 
you have indi-ated, and ltr, Mccabe has confirmed, that you
are auth-orized by Supervisor Mclaughlin to seek advice on the
supervisor's renilr.- 
Because at present there is no specific
;;;;;;;;i"i 
decision p..Ji"s, thii aavice is informar onrv'2
OUESTTON
Is supervisor Mclaughlin, as an independent contractor-for'
and ai a rLcipient of iniorne from, the current lessees and future
1 Gor"r.,..nt code Sections 81ooo-91015. AII statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated' 
Commission

ie6oo, et "eq.- AII references to regulations are to Title 2'

provided by an opinion or fornal written advice'
(Section bsrfs; Regulation 18329 (c) ' )
428 J Street,Suite 800 ● P.0.Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804´ 0807 0 (916)322´ 5660


Our File No. 
■-9■-497
Page 2
owners of property adjacent to property lrhich is the subject of a
use pernit application, disqualified from participating in the use
pernit application decision?
CONCLUS IONS
The supervisor is disqualified if the decision wiII result in
a materiaL financial effect to a source of incorne to hin of $250
or more during the twelve months prior to the decision.
FACTS
Your client, Site constructors, fnc. has applied for a use
pernj.t to enlarge its commercial property. If approved. the
application would have permitted site Constructors to construct
buildings on its prernises to replace older buildings, as weII as
construiting an addition to an existing building. 
Additionally,
Site constructors woul-d be reguired to install a perimeter fence
around the premises and to plant shrubs along the main highway.
Adjacent to the comnercial property is a parcel of. farming
property, currently leased to the MeIine and Rabo fanilies 
(rrthe
two families"). 
You belj.eved, and Mr. Mccabe conf irrned, that the
two farnilies operated the property as an incorporated business.
Members of the two fahilies have exercised an option to purchase
the farming property, and the transaction will be concluded on
Decenber 2, L99L.
Supervisor Ed McLaughlin has done work on the property for
the two familj.es for several years, work which generates between
$8 , 000 and S25,000 annually.
After having its application for the permit to expand a
nonconforning use reiected by the county's pJ.anning cornmission,
Site constructors appealed to the Board of Supervj.sors. At a
regular rneeti,ng of the board on September L7, L99L, the board
voted 2 - 2 on a motion to uphold the plannj.ng commissi'on's
deci.sion. Upon advice from the county counsel, Supervisor
Mcl,aughli,n did not participate in the decision because of a
perceived potential disgualifying conflict of interest and v/iIl
tontinue not to participate unl'ess advised otherwise. 
S j-te
Constructors may seek recons j,deration of the matter by the Board
of Supervisors if Supervisor Mcl.aughlin is permitted to
participate.
ANALYS I S
Section 8710o prohibits a public officiaL from making,
participating in making, or otherwise using his or her official
position to influence a governmental decisj'on in which the


Our File NO. I-91-497
Page 3
officiaL has a financiaL interest. 
A public official 
is
financially interested in a decision if it is reasonably
foreseeable that the decision wiII have a nateriaL financial
effect that is distinguishable fron the effect on the public
general.ly, on among other things, any source of income aggregating
two hundred and fifty doll-ars ($250) or nore in value within
t$reLve rnonths prior to the time when the decision is made-
(section 87103 (c) . )
!1r. McLaughlin, as an elected county supervj-sor, is a public
official 
(section 82048), and the two families' constj-tute an
incorne source aggregating 5250 or more to hirn during the twelve
month period prior io the time of the decision on the use permit
appIJ.cltion. Therefore, Mr. Mclaughlin must continue to
diiquafify himself fron participation in the use permit.
appiication decision if the financial effect of the decision on
tirl two fanilies wilI be both foreseeable and naterial.
Foreseeabi l itv
Disqualifj.cation is required from a decision when the
of f icial'-s economic interest wilL be foreseeably and materially
financially affected. 
Whether or not the consequences of .a
decision aie reasonably foreseeable at the time the decision is
made depends on the falts of each particular case' An effect is
considered reasonable foreseeable if there is a substantial
liketihood that it will occur. while the Act seeks to prevent
both actual conflicts of interest and the appearance of a possj.ble
conflict of interest (witt v. Morrov, (L977]. 70 CaL.App'3d 817),
certainty is not required. However, an effect that is only a mere
possibillty is not ieasonably foreseeable. (In re Thorner (1975)
i Fppc ops. rsa; Downev cares v. Downev connunitv Development
Comm. ( Lg87l L96 caI.APp.3d 983, 989.)
Because you have indicated that the two fami.l'ies' property j's
situated irnneiiately adjacent to the property which is.the subject
of the S j.te Constructora' appJ'ication for the use perrnit,
Supervisor McLaughlin may not participate in decisi'ons on the
peimit if it is ieasonably foreseeable that the effect of the
decision on the two fanilies will be material. 
The proximity of
the property leased/o\"rned by an incorne source to the supervisor is
such'thit iL would appear that the foreseeability requisite has
been satisfied. 
If the application were approved, it is
foreseeable that the permitted use, in such close proxi.mity to the
two fanj-1ies' property, could increase - or decrease - the fair
market vatue oi their property. It is also foreseeable that the
two familj,es' incorne or other tangible or intangible assets or
liabili.ties 
could be affected.


Our File No. I-91-497
Page 4
Materialitv
Disqua 1if ication requires both foreseeability and a material
effect ori an official's 
financial interest. 
The Cornmission has
adopted regulations which provide guidetines to deternine whether
the effect of a governmental decision is material.
It has been suggested, but not confirned, that the two
fanilies which constitute the source of income to Supervisor
Mclaughlin apparently operate the farrning property as.a business'
where a businlss entity in which the official 
has a financial
interest is directly involved in a decision before the official's
agency, the official 
may not participate. 
(Regulation
L87O2-.1 (a).) 
Here, however, neither the supervisor nor the two
families are directly involved in the application for the pernit
to expand a nonconforming use at Site constructors' property'
The Act also requires an official to disqualify himself or
herself from participation in governnental decisions which
indirectlv haie a material financial effect on a business entity
in rrtict rr" or she is financially interested. whether the effect
of a decision on a business entity that is not directly involved
in the decision is material depends on the financial size of the
business entity. 
Regulation La7o2.2 describes the standards,
oii"a "" the financiil sire of the business entity, to determine
whether the effect of the decision is material'
wecannotdeterminethemagnitudeofthefinancj.aleffecton
the two farnilies' property that would be caused by the decisions
o" tfl. nonconformi.tq use pernit application, since you have not
provided any facts witn rlspect ta- this issue' we must leave this
i."t".l 
-a"ilrminat ion of materiality to you within the guidelines
provided bY Regulation L87 02 ,2 .
For example, even though Site constructors' property' the
suujeci-of thl appJ,ication, is innediately adjacent. to !h?-two
fanilies' 
property, the magnitude of-the proposed change-rn use
resulting ?rorn tne applicaiion in relation to the two families'
pi"p..iy'..y 
ne retaLively minor, so that the financial impact on
'ine'two' fanilies's properiy or business may arso be. ninor'. 
(Trqcv
Advice Letter, No. r-gi-oai.) 
In other woids, if the constructi-on
on Site constructor=' l.op.tty wiIl only minimally irnpact'. in a
;;=;ai;"-;; 
negative .innlt, 
Lhe two farnities' propertv' the
iinancial effeit of the expansion nay be mj'ninaI'
If the two families constitute an incone source to the
=rrp".ii=J.-ut inal"iat,ars, the impact of the use pgrrnit 
. .
;;;ii;;ai;" 
decision wourd be con!idered rnaterj'ar if: (1) the two
;ffiiii;;; 
ir,"or", investnents, assets or riabiritj'es 
(other than
;;;i-;;;p";Ivt 
';" inpacted bv sr'ooo or nore (Reeuration


!a7o2.6(a) ) or (2) i-t the two fa:nilies' property is irnpacted by
Slo,ooo or nore in fair narket value or S1roo0 or more in rental
vatue (Regulation 18702.3(a) (3)), or (3) if the use of, or the
rent or lease for, two famities' Ieased property will change as a
result of the decision. 
(Regulation 18702.4.)
In your letter you have surnrnarily indicated that the effect
of the uie permi.t application decision wiII not irnpact the two
farnilies in the manner specj.f ied by either Regulation L8702.2(g),
or Regulation 18702.6. we note that if the two families operate
the firming property as a business, thereby making Supervisor
Mclaughlin's- source of income a business entity, then Regulation
18702.6 would not be applicable. If the two fanrilies' do not
operate the farning property as a business, or do not otherwise
ply Supervisor llcl-aughlin from a business account, then the tl'/o
iamifiL= are an income source to the supervisor as an individual,
making Regulation :-8702.2 inapplicable. If one is applicable, the
other is not.
In any event, we are unable to either confirm or deny your
concLus j,oni with respect to the two regulations. 
(Fitzqerald
Advice Letter, No. I-90-469.) The conmission does not act as a
fact-finder, and can provide advice only to the extent that
sufficient facts are provided, that the facts are correct, and
that a1I material facts have been presented. (In re oqlesbv
(1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71.) Because you have not offered any facts to
support your conclusions, we decline to confirm your analysis, but
notL tnai you have correctly identified the regulations that are
applicable for the required analysis. we are not in a position to
dllernine whether or not the facts support your conclusions,
because no facts have been provided.
I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you
requested.. If you have further questions regarding this matter,
or wish to supplement your advice request with additionaJ- facts,
please contact me at (916) 322-59OL.
Our Fi■e No. I-9■-497
Page 5
Sincerely,
Scott HaIlabrin
Jonathan S. Rothman
Counsel, LegaI D ivi s ion
